translated: When Herod the king heard that the wise men had come to Jerusalem, he was troubled. He asked them about the star they had seen in the east and where the child was born, so that he too could go and worship him. Then he sent them to Bethlehem, saying, "Go and search carefully for the child, and when you have found him, bring me word, that I may come and worship him also." After hearing the king, they departed, and the star which they had seen in the east went before them until it came and stood over where the child was. When they saw the star, they rejoiced with great joy. And entering the house, they saw the child with Mary his mother, and they fell down and worshipped him. Then, having been warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they departed to their own country by another way.

ref: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
text: Bala, mam han tara Beelezebule sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, gee yamam nɛreba la me tari la ani sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi? Bala zuo, ya mɛhi zahereba la en wan gakɛ ya.
translated: Bala, if you cast out Beelzebub by the power of Beelzebub, how can you do it? Come, speak the words in the house.

ref: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
text: Ti la iŋɛ fii wa ti nɛreba la n ze' la paaɛ Pita zi'an yele en yeti, “Yelemɛŋerɛ fum dela ba ayima. Fum tugum n pa'alɛ.”
translated: When he arrived at the house, he saw Peter's shadow and said, "The sick were healed by his shadow. They were healed when they touched it."
